label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 10

extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco won the 31.1km Alicante TT by 48 seconds over Roglic, extending his GC lead from 1:12 to 2:41
tactical_patterns: Evenepoel averaged 55.7 km/h versus 54.4 for Cavagna, revealing 1.3 km/h delta separates elite TT from merely strong performance.
Roglic gained 12 seconds on benchmark Cavagna while Mas lost over a minute, exposing Mas as vulnerable on any TT course.
Rodriguez in white jersey rode for Evenepoel rather than contesting, UAE prioritizing GC defense over young rider classification.
Almeida lost 20 seconds using evacuation lane, suggesting course reconnaissance failures cost Ineos potential podium positions.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: 48-second margin on 31km flat TT signals ability to extend leads exponentially in any future chrono stage
rider_signals.MAS Enric: negative: losing over 60 seconds to Roglic on pan-flat course confirms he cannot defend GC against time-trialists in third week
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: described as rekindling Giro-level TT form while moving to 5th overall, making him podium threat if form continues
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: 48-second loss reduced him from 1:53 to 2:41 deficit but stayed second, suggesting he peaked TT form but Evenepoel untouchable
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: negative: course navigation error cost 20 seconds in evacuation lane, revealing poor recon by Ineos staff
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: self-described as upset after finishing 1:17 down, indicating his climbing prowess doesn't translate to TT discipline
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remy: positive: held hot seat until late starters, proving 54.4 km/h benchmark on flat TT remains elite non-specialist standard
